items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

This is a **sub-tracking issue** for the MPG (Management Plane Generator / TypeSpec) migration of the **Azure Front Door** management SDK (`Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor`), part of the larger [Azure Mgmt Generator migration #53864](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864).

**Package location:** `sdk/frontdoor/Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor/`

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Summary</summary>

| Field | Details |
|---|---|
| **Type** | Internal tracking issue — MPG (TypeSpec) migration |
| **Package** | `Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor` |
| **SDK directory** | `sdk/frontdoor/Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor/` |
| **Parent issue** | [#53864 – Azure Mgmt Generator migration](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864) |
| **Filed by** | Internal team member (`haiyuazhang`) |

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔧 Migration Checklist</summary>

- [ ] Locate or create the TypeSpec spec for `Microsoft.Network/frontdoor` in [azure-rest-api-specs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs)
- [ ] Set up `tsp-location.yaml` in `sdk/frontdoor/Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor/`
- [ ] Run `azsdk_package_generate_code` to generate the SDK from TypeSpec
- [ ] Run `dotnet build` to verify the generated code compiles
- [ ] Run `dotnet test` to validate tests pass
- [ ] Run pre-commit checks (`Export-API.ps1`, `dotnet format`, snippet updates)
- [ ] Identify and address breaking changes (use `@CodeGenSuppress`, `@clientName`, `hierarchyBuilding`, etc.)
- [ ] Update the CHANGELOG.md
- [ ] Submit a PR referencing this issue

</details>

<details>
<summary>🛠️ Helpful Resources</summary>

- [MPG migration skill / playbook](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/mpg-sdk-migration/SKILL.md)
- [Mitigating breaking changes guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/mitigate-breaking-changes/SKILL.md)
- [AGENTS.md – repo overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/AGENTS.md)
- [Local SDK workflow instructions](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/common/instructions/azsdk-tools/local-sdk-workflow.instructions.md)
- [API compatibility guidelines (breaking changes)](https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/docs/coding-guidelines/breaking-change-rules.md)
- Parent tracking: [Completed RPs #55080](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/55080)

</details>

<details>
<summary>💡 Debugging / Known Issues for MPG Migrations</summary>

- **Missing `using DiagnosticScope scope = ...`**: Known bug where sync methods in generated Collection/Resource/MockableResource files are missing the diagnostic scope line — watch for `CS0103` build errors after generation.
- **`@`@clientName`` on ARM resource types**: The mgmt emitter does NOT rename Resource/Collection/Data types via `@`@clientName`` — only plain model types are renamed. Use `CodeGenType` customization instead if renaming is needed.
- **Wire path attributes**: If the existing package uses `WirePathAttribute`, enable `enable-wire-path-attribute: true` in `tspconfig.yaml`.
- **FlattenPropertyVisitor crash**: If flattened properties exist and models lack a public constructor, ensure the generator has the `FirstOrDefault` fix (see [known generator bug](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Visitors/FlattenPropertyVisitor.cs)).

</details>
